query: My eww bar is acting weird — variables are stale. I want to kill the daemon and start it fresh. eww runs from a systemd user service. What's the right way to restart it?
expected_behavior: Routes to SYSTEMD.md (and the systemd warning at the top of the Debug Workflow)
Warns AGAINST `eww kill` / `eww daemon` when a systemd user service with Restart=always manages eww
Explains that manually killing it lets systemd relaunch its own daemon while the manual one also starts, producing two daemons and duplicate / double bars
Prescribes `systemctl --user restart eww.service` for a full restart and `eww reload` for a config-only change
notes: Guards against the #1 desktop-eww trap: a naive answer says 'just eww kill && eww daemon', which on a systemd-managed instance spawns a second daemon and gives the user double bars. The skill knows to detect the service first and use systemctl restart.
